title: Exploring the data sets
text: flyer available GitHub, make way CRAN eventually. can installed via either following commands. explore data ’ll load couple packages handling (sf, dplyr), plus slew packages plotting ggplot2 (ggnewscale, ggrepel, ggspatial). might seem onerous install , bet see ’ll quite excited . ’ll modify number graticules right bat. Note pretty function always return requested n, wrap small helper.
code: install.packages('devtools') devtools::install_github('sagesteppe/flyer')  # remotes is very similar and a good alternative for this use case. install.packages('remotes')  remotes::install_github('sagesteppe/flyer') library(flyer) library(dplyr) # for general data handling library(sf) # for spatial data  library(ggplot2) # all for plotting the data  library(ggnewscale) # for mapping multiple variables to an aesthetic.  library(ggrepel) # for text based labels which move to minimize overlaps.  library(ggspatial) # compasses and scale bars. graticNo <- function(polygon, nx, ny){      bb <- round(st_bbox(polygon), 1)   if(all(missing(nx) & missing(ny))) {nx <- 5;ny <- 5} else {     if(all(missing(nx) & ! missing(ny))) {nx <- ny} else {       if(all(missing(ny) & ! missing(nx))) {ny <- nx}     }    }     xbreaks <- pretty(seq(bb[1], bb[3], length.out = nx), nx)   ybreaks <- pretty(seq(bb[2], bb[4], length.out = ny), ny)      return(     list(       x = xbreaks, y = ybreaks     )   ) }  brks <- graticNo(polygon = places, nx = 4, ny = 4)  theme_nautical <- function() {   theme(          aspect.ratio = 4/3,     text = element_text(family = "Optima"),     axis.title = element_text(colour = "#222823"),     axis.text = element_text(colour = "#575A5E", face = "italic"),     axis.text.x = element_text(hjust= 1, angle=45),     axis.ticks = element_line(colour = "#575A5E"),          plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),     plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 1, face = 'italic'),     plot.caption = element_text(color = '#575A5E', face = 'italic'),          panel.background = element_rect(fill = "#F4F7F5"),     panel.border = element_rect(colour = NA, fill = NA),     panel.grid.major = element_line(colour = "#A7A2A9", linetype = 'dotted', linewidth = 0.25),     panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),          legend.background = element_rect(fill = '#FEF9F3', color = '#A7A2A9'),     legend.text = element_text(size = 5),     legend.title = element_text(size = 7, hjust = 0.5),      legend.title.position = 'top',     legend.key.size = unit(1,"line"),     legend.position = "bottom",     legend.spacing = unit(0.1, "line")   ) }  bb <-st_bbox(     c(xmin = -121, xmax = -105, ymin = 19, ymax = 34.5),  crs = st_crs(4326) )

title: Exploring the data sets
text: places visited collectors can loaded via places, route took via route. really whole point package — , spoiler alert, ’re simple! pull places route, let’s pull land data set context plot .

title: Exploring the data sets
text: can read polygons depicting land Natural Earth, via rnaturalearth package. love package’s functionality, even get real forgetful API calls (theme argument?).  playing around data today, don’t want different countries drawn separately, can union .
code: head(land) #> Simple feature collection with 2 features and 1 field #> Geometry type: MULTIPOLYGON #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: -120.8977 ymin: 19 xmax: -103 ymax: 35.30912 #> Geodetic CRS:  WGS 84 #> # A tibble: 2 × 2 #>   name                                                                  geometry #>   <chr>                                                       <MULTIPOLYGON [°]> #> 1 Mexico                   (((-113.0957 29.0635, -113.0897 29.06302, -113.0823 … #> 2 United States of America (((-119.3816 34.01122, -119.3923 34.00631, -119.4113…  ggplot() +    geom_sf(data = land, fill = '#F8F6F0') +    theme_nautical() +   labs(title = 'land') +    coord_sf(xlim = c(bb[1], bb[3]), ylim = c(bb[2], bb[4])) land <- st_union(land)  m <- ggplot() +    geom_sf(data = land, fill = '#F8F6F0') +    theme_nautical() +    labs(title = 'st_union(land)') +    coord_sf(xlim = c(bb[1], bb[3]), ylim = c(bb[2], bb[4]))   m

title: Exploring the data sets
text: places seem like ’ll better treated text labels — can apply ggrepel::geom_text_repel, nudge avoid conflicts plot elements.  package attached, can just start using data — ’s currently held promise. words, don’t need call data(object) data sets; can use directly (example, calling head(object)). ’ll use direct approach remainder vignette.
code: data(places) head(places) #> Simple feature collection with 6 features and 6 fields #> Geometry type: POINT #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: -117.1852 ymin: 22.88 xmax: -109.4243 ymax: 32.71662 #> Geodetic CRS:  WGS 84 #>                   location_espanol                 location_english collect #> 1                        San Diego                        San Diego   FALSE #> 2               Bahía de Magdalena                    Magdalena Bay   FALSE #> 3                   Cabo San Lucas                   Cape San Lucas    TRUE #> 4                 Bahia Pulmo Reef                       Cabo Pulmo    TRUE #> 5                  Punta Pescadero                  Punta Pescadero   FALSE #> 6 Punta Lobos, Isla Espiritu Santo Punta Lobos, Isla Espiritu Santo    TRUE #>   real_site date_arrive date_depart                   geometry #> 1        NA  1940-03-13  1940-03-14 POINT (-117.1852 32.71662) #> 2        NA  1940-03-16  1940-03-16 POINT (-111.9988 24.58293) #> 3      TRUE  1940-03-17  1940-03-18     POINT (-109.903 22.88) #> 4     FALSE  1940-03-19  1940-03-18 POINT (-109.4243 23.43779) #> 5      TRUE  1940-03-19  1940-03-20  POINT (-109.6971 23.7965) #> 6      TRUE  1940-03-20  1940-03-20    POINT (-110.293 24.459)  m <- m +    geom_sf(data = places)  +   labs(title = 'places', subtitle = '+ land') +    coord_sf(xlim = c(bb[1], bb[3]), ylim = c(bb[2], bb[4]))  #> Coordinate system already present. #> ℹ Adding new coordinate system, which will replace the existing one. m m <- m +    coord_sf(xlim = c(-118, -106), ylim = c(22, 31)) +   ggrepel::geom_text_repel(     data = places,     aes(label = location_espanol, geometry = geometry),     stat = "sf_coordinates",     size = 2.5     ) +       # now let's add in our customized graticules too.    scale_x_continuous(breaks = brks$x) +   scale_y_continuous(breaks = brks$y) +    theme_nautical() +       labs(     x = NULL, y = NULL,     title = 'geom_text_repel(places)',      subtitle = '+ land + places'     ) #> Coordinate system already present. #> ℹ Adding new coordinate system, which will replace the existing one.  m

title: Exploring the data sets
text: route object pretty minimal, relevant attributes can brought joining places. data start end trip included — entries near Santa Barbara departure return Monterey — focuses Gulf California. Basically, including much data make package cumbersome fit CRAN. mind, maximum useful map area reaches San Diego north.  Now let’s add topography make land seem natural. ’ll also ignore administrative borders. Note ’re going back drawing board control order layers added map. ’ll still overwrite variable m.  can plot bathymetry data like . obviously rename something like depth :) Alternatively, scale can used topography bathymetry together, shown . ’ll use divergent scale, makes sense . Another cool interpretation continuous scale counts everything 0 4,300 feet adds difference topography data set. — much liking — convert bathymetry polylines polygons use color whole ocean, darker areas deeper hues blue.
code: head(route) #> Simple feature collection with 6 features and 1 field #> Geometry type: LINESTRING #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: -113.4777 ymin: 22.60915 xmax: -109.2605 ymax: 28.99745 #> Geodetic CRS:  WGS 84 #> # A tibble: 6 × 2 #>   destination                                                           geometry #>   <chr>                                                         <LINESTRING [°]> #> 1 Agiabampo                    (-109.98 27.00303, -109.9776 26.99705, -109.9767… #> 2 Amatorajada, San José Island (-110.3415 24.19858, -110.342 24.18917, -110.344… #> 3 Angeles Bay                  (-112.8615 28.47699, -112.8717 28.48583, -112.88… #> 4 Cabo Pulmo                   (-109.8743 22.86407, -109.8674 22.86868, -109.85… #> 5 Caimancito, La Paz           (-110.2735 24.38412, -110.2872 24.38077, -110.30… #> 6 Cape San Lucas               (-112.5084 24.37574, -112.4991 24.3553, -112.489…  route <- left_join(   route,   st_drop_geometry(places),   by = c('destination' = 'location_english')   ) |>   relocate(geometry, .after = last_col()) head(route) #> Simple feature collection with 6 features and 6 fields #> Geometry type: LINESTRING #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: -113.4777 ymin: 22.60915 xmax: -109.2605 ymax: 28.99745 #> Geodetic CRS:  WGS 84 #> # A tibble: 6 × 7 #>   destination         location_espanol collect real_site date_arrive date_depart #>   <chr>               <chr>            <lgl>   <lgl>     <date>      <date>      #> 1 Agiabampo           Agiabampo        TRUE    FALSE     1940-04-11  1940-04-11  #> 2 Amatorajada, San J… Isolote Cayo, I… FALSE   TRUE      1940-03-23  1940-03-23  #> 3 Angeles Bay         Bahía del Los A… TRUE    FALSE     1940-04-01  1940-04-01  #> 4 Cabo Pulmo          Bahia Pulmo Reef TRUE    FALSE     1940-03-19  1940-03-18  #> 5 Caimancito, La Paz  Caimancito, La … TRUE    TRUE      1940-03-21  1940-03-22  #> 6 Cape San Lucas      Cabo San Lucas   TRUE    TRUE      1940-03-17  1940-03-18  #> # ℹ 1 more variable: geometry <LINESTRING [°]> date_scale <- as.Date(quantile(as.numeric(route$date_arrive), na.rm = T, probs = c(0.1, 0.5, 0.9))) dates <- c(   expression(paste("March ", 16^th)),    expression(paste("Mar. ", 28^th)),    expression(paste("April ", 11^th))   ) bb <- st_bbox(   c(xmin = -114, xmax = -108.5, ymin = 22.5, ymax = 30.5),   crs = st_crs(4326) )  # we have to crop the places data set or ggrepel will move places outside the # coord_sf down into the plot anyways  places <- st_crop(places, bb)  m <- ggplot()  +   geom_sf(data = land, fill = '#F8F6F0') +    theme_nautical() +   geom_sf(data = route, aes(color = date_arrive)) +    # scale_color_date() +  # alteratively just use this and default labels.    scale_color_continuous('Date',       breaks = date_scale,       labels = dates       )  +    coord_sf(xlim = c(bb[1], bb[3]), ylim = c(bb[2], bb[4])) +       labs(     title = 'route',      subtitle = '+ land')     m m <- ggplot() +    geom_sf(data = land, fill = '#F8F6F0') +    geom_sf(data = topography, lwd = 0.1) +    geom_sf(data = route, aes(color = date_arrive)) +   scale_color_date('Date') +    coord_sf(xlim = c(bb[1], bb[3]), ylim = c(bb[2], bb[4])) +     # we are going to shift to adding 'backing' to the labels this makes them easier to read   ggrepel::geom_label_repel(     data = places,     aes(label = location_espanol, geometry = geometry),     stat = "sf_coordinates",     alpha = 0.7, # make the backing more transparent     label.size = NA, # remove the backing borders     label.padding = 0.1, # reduce space between label borders and text     size = 2.5 # make the font smaller.      ) +      scale_color_continuous('Date',       breaks = date_scale,       labels = dates       ) +       # now let's add in our customized graticules too.    scale_x_continuous(breaks = brks$x) +   scale_y_continuous(breaks = brks$y) +    theme_nautical() +    labs(     x = NULL, y = NULL,     title = 'elevation',      subtitle = '+ route + places')  #> Scale for colour is already present. #> Adding another scale for colour, which will replace the existing scale.  m #> Warning in st_point_on_surface.sfc(sf::st_zm(x)): st_point_on_surface may not #> give correct results for longitude/latitude data ggplot() +    geom_sf(data = land, fill = '#F8F6F0') +    geom_sf(data = bathymetry, aes(color = elevation), lwd = 0.4)  +   theme_nautical() +    coord_sf(xlim = c(bb[1], bb[3]), ylim = c(bb[2], bb[4])) +    labs(title = 'bathymetry', subtitle = '+ land') head(bathymetry) #> Simple feature collection with 6 features and 1 field #> Geometry type: LINESTRING #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: -121 ymin: 19.59502 xmax: -117.0035 ymax: 31.94066 #> Geodetic CRS:  WGS 84 #>     elevation                       geometry #> 1       -4000 LINESTRING (-121 31.91037, ... #> 1.1     -4000 LINESTRING (-121 29.55895, ... #> 1.2     -4000 LINESTRING (-121 26.51376, ... #> 1.3     -4000 LINESTRING (-121 27.20785, ... #> 1.4     -4000 LINESTRING (-121 21.41284, ... #> 1.5     -4000 LINESTRING (-121 21.86726, ...  ggplot() +    geom_sf(data = land, fill = '#F8F6F0') +    geom_sf(data = bathymetry, aes(color = elevation), lwd = 0.4) +    geom_sf(data = topography, aes(color = elevation), lwd = 0.4) +    scale_color_distiller('Elevation', palette = "Spectral") +      ggnewscale::new_scale_color() +     geom_sf(data = places) +     geom_sf(data = route, aes(color = date_arrive)) +   labs(title = 'topography', subtitle = '+ land + bathymetry')+   scale_color_continuous('Date',       breaks = date_scale,       labels = dates       ) +    theme_nautical() +    coord_sf(xlim = c(bb[1], bb[3]), ylim = c(bb[2], bb[4]))

title: Exploring the data sets
text: earlier data sets loosely related book, next two aren’t related — can useful cartography. simple landcover classification data set available landcover. also include quick colors help mapping classes.  Information protected areas Mexico also included.
code: ggplot() +    geom_sf(data = landcover, aes(fill = class), color = NA) +    scale_fill_manual('Class', values = lc_pal, breaks = names(lc_pal[c(1:8, 9)])) +    theme_nautical() +    labs(title = 'landcover', subtitle = '+ lc_pal') +    guides(fill = guide_legend(nrow = 3)) +    coord_sf(xlim = c(bb[1], bb[3]), ylim = c(bb[2], bb[4])) protected <- st_crop(protected, bb)  ggplot() +    geom_sf(data = land, fill = '#F8F6F0') +    geom_sf(     data = protected,      aes(fill = reserve_type),      alpha = 0.4) +    scale_fill_manual('Reserve', values = c('Terrestrial' = '#417B5A', 'Marine' = '#DA7635')) +    ggrepel::geom_text_repel(     data = protected,     aes(label = name, geometry = geometry),     stat = "sf_coordinates",     size = 2.5     ) +    labs(x = NULL, y = NULL, title = 'protected', subtitle = '+ land') +     theme_nautical()  +    coord_sf(xlim = c(bb[1], bb[3]), ylim = c(bb[2], bb[4]))

title: Exploring the data sets
text: can make OK map using details .
code: ggplot() +       # the stage   geom_sf(data = landcover, aes(fill = class)) +    scale_fill_manual('Class', values = lc_pal) +    geom_sf(data = land, fill = '#F8F6F0', alpha = 0.5) +  # helps to dull the landcover for this map.    guides(fill="none") +     geom_sf(data = bathymetry, aes(color = elevation), lwd = 0.25) +    geom_sf(data = topography,  aes(color = elevation), lwd = 0.25, color = 'black') +  #  scale_color_distiller('Elevation', palette = "Spectral") +   guides(color='none') +    ggnewscale::new_scale_colour() +       # the story    geom_sf(data = route, aes(color = date_arrive)) +     scale_color_continuous('Date',       breaks = date_scale,       labels = dates       ) +     geom_sf(data = places, shape = 5, size = 1.5, color = '#222823') +      ggrepel::geom_label_repel(     data = places,     aes(label = location_espanol, geometry = geometry),     stat = "sf_coordinates",     alpha = 0.7, # make the backing more transparent     label.size = NA, # remove the backing borders     label.padding = 0.1, # reduce space between label borders and text     size = 2.5,# make the font smaller.      fill = '#F4F7F5'     ) +      # the ambiance.     coord_sf(xlim = c(bb[1], bb[3]), ylim = c(bb[2], bb[4]), crs = 4326) +    annotation_scale(bar_cols = c('#222823', '#F4F7F5'))  +    annotation_north_arrow(which_north = "true", style = north_arrow_nautical) +    theme_nautical() +   labs(title = 'A map', subtitle = 'Ricketts <3 giardiniera', x = NULL, y = NULL) #> Warning in st_point_on_surface.sfc(sf::st_zm(x)): st_point_on_surface may not #> give correct results for longitude/latitude data rm(graticNo, brks, theme_nautical, route, places, bb, landcover, lc_pal, land,    protected, bathymetry, topography)

title: An interactive map
text: ExploreDataSets vignette walks building static ggplot2 maps collection route. One stated advantages data set follows sequential series events, lends just well interactive treatment — reader can pan Sea Cortez, click stop see name Spanish English, trace Western Flyer’s path day day. build leaflet map time slider (via leaflet.extras2::addTimeslider) reveals collection stops progressively arrival date. full route stays drawn background visual context, colored arrival date, play/scrub control lets reader replay expedition day day.
code: library(flyer) library(sf) library(dplyr) library(leaflet) library(leaflet.extras2)

title: An interactive map
text: route object carries destination field, join places bring arrival date onto segment — pattern used static vignette.
code: route <- left_join(   route,   st_drop_geometry(places),   by = c('destination' = 'location_english') ) |>   relocate(geometry, .after = last_col())

title: An interactive map
text: use continuous palette date_arrive route segments, small handful quantile-based tick marks legend labels stay readable. notes interaction: label small text appears hover, popup richer HTML card appears click, time slider (top-right map, knit) two-handle range slider filters markers shown start end date. addTimeslider expects single time attribute per feature — hood — caps slider count distinct times minus one. stops share arrival date (e.g. two places dated March 19), synthesize per-feature unique timestamps adding small seconds offset within -date groups. keeps slider stranding last features. also set timeStrLength = 10 date portion displayed slider readout.
code:
date_domain <- as.numeric(route$date_arrive) pal <- colorNumeric(palette = 'viridis', domain = date_domain, na.color = 'transparent') legend_breaks <- quantile(date_domain, probs = c(0.1, 0.5, 0.9), na.rm = TRUE) legend_labels <- format(as.Date(legend_breaks, origin = '1970-01-01'), '%b %d') places <- places |> arrange(date_arrive) |> group_by(date_arrive) |> mutate(seq = row_number() - 1L) |> ungroup() |> mutate( arrive_str = format(date_arrive, '%b %d, %Y'), depart_str = format(date_depart, '%b %d, %Y'), stay = ifelse( date_arrive == date_depart, arrive_str, paste(arrive_str, '–', depart_str) ), time = format(as.POSIXct(date_arrive) + seq, '%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S') ) |> select(-seq) leaflet(width = '100%', height = '650px') |> addProviderTiles(providers$Esri.OceanBasemap) |> addPolylines( data = route, color = ~pal(as.numeric(date_arrive)), weight = 3, opacity = 0.9, label = ~paste0(destination, ' · ', format(date_arrive, '%b %d')) ) |> addTimeslider( data = places, radius = 5, color = '#222823', fillColor = '#D35269', fillOpacity = 0.9, weight = 1, label = ~paste0(location_english, ' · ', format(date_arrive, '%b %d')), popup = ~paste0( '', location_english, '
', '', location_espanol, '
', '
', 'Arrived: ', arrive_str, '
', 'Departed: ', depart_str, '
', 'Stay: ', stay, '
', 'Collection site: ', ifelse(collect, 'yes', 'no') ), options = timesliderOptions( timeAttribute = 'time', timeStrLength = 10, range = TRUE, alwaysShowDate = TRUE, position = 'topright' ) ) |> addLegend( position = 'bottomright', colors = pal(legend_breaks), labels = legend_labels, title = 'Date arrived', opacity = 0.9 ) #> Loading required namespace: yyjsonr

title: A dark-mode map
text: ExploreDataSets vignette builds static map light “nautical” theme. redo map dark mode, borrowing palette package’s hex sticker: navy night sky, warm orange land, purple route, star markers collection stops.
code: library(flyer) library(sf) library(dplyr) library(ggplot2) library(ggrepel) library(ggspatial)

title: A dark-mode map
text: Layered “bottom top”: bathymetry contours cool blues, land warm oranges (gold border lighter fill), topography contours mint, route purple gradient arrival date, places gold star-glyphs cream labels dark backing.
code: ggplot() +    # bathymetry from deep ocean to shallower shelves   geom_sf(     data = bathymetry,     aes(color = elevation),     lwd = 0.35   ) +   scale_color_gradient(     'Depth (m)',     low = sticker_pal$night,     high = sticker_pal$ocean_hi,     guide = 'none'   ) +    # land -- warm orange fill with a coral border   geom_sf(     data = land,     fill = sticker_pal$land_light,     color = sticker_pal$coral,     linewidth = 0.25   ) +    # topography -- mint to cream gradient by elevation   ggnewscale::new_scale_color() +   geom_sf(     data = topography,     aes(color = elevation),     linewidth = 0.45   ) +   scale_color_gradient(     'Elevation (m)',     low = sticker_pal$cream,     high = alpha(sticker_pal$mint, 0.55),     guide = 'none'   ) +    # route -- purple gradient along arrival date   ggnewscale::new_scale_color() +   geom_sf(     data = route,     aes(color = date_arrive),     linewidth = 0.8   ) +   scale_color_gradient(     'Date',     low = sticker_pal$purple,     high = sticker_pal$lavender,     breaks = date_scale,     labels = date_lbls   ) +    # places -- rendered as star glyphs   geom_sf_text(     data = places,     label = '★',   # unicode filled star     color = sticker_pal$star_pink,     size = 6   ) +   ggrepel::geom_label_repel(     data = places,     aes(label = location_espanol, geometry = geometry),     stat = 'sf_coordinates',     size = 2.4,     color = sticker_pal$cream,     fill = alpha(sticker_pal$night, 0.75),     label.size = NA,     label.padding = 0.15,     segment.color = alpha(sticker_pal$star_gold, 0.6)   ) +    # ambiance   coord_sf(xlim = c(bb[1], bb[3]), ylim = c(bb[2], bb[4]), crs = 4326) +   annotation_scale(     bar_cols = c(sticker_pal$gold, sticker_pal$night),     text_col = sticker_pal$cream,     line_col = sticker_pal$gold   ) +   annotation_north_arrow(     which_north = 'true',     style = north_arrow_nautical(       fill = c(sticker_pal$cream, sticker_pal$night),       line_col = sticker_pal$gold,       text_col = sticker_pal$cream     )   ) +   theme_nautical_night() +   labs(     x = NULL, y = NULL,     title = 'flyer',     subtitle = 'Sea of Cortez, spring 1940',     caption = '"it is advisable to look from the tide pool to the stars\nand back to the tidepool again"'   )

title: Example geospatial data for cartography
text: Geographic data Steinbeck Ricketts’ 1940 expedition Sea Cortez. original R package, includes data-cleaning code; Python package contains data.

title: Example geospatial data for cartography

code: install.packages('devtools') devtools::install_github('sagesteppe/flyer')  # remotes is very similar and a good alternative for this use case. install.packages('remotes')  remotes::install_github('sagesteppe/flyer') library(flyer)

title: Example geospatial data for cartography
text: original author marine ecologist, nautically inclined; rather, appreciate journey philosophical exploration ecology. goal package provide readily available data set cartographic activities — particular, one composed point linestring geometries rather polygons typical choropleth applications (North Carolina SIDS data set ships sf). advantage data set follows sequential series events, lending story maps, interactive maps, animations. Consider package much alive: happy merge increasingly accurate collection localities routes. read Log Sea Cortez, Sea Cortez: Leisurely Journal Travel Research, true catalogs voyage. also quickly poked Brusca 2020 — wonderful read served definitive source locations. ‘route’ Western Flyer liberally digitized redrawn Penguin Classics 1995 edition Log Sea Cortez, accuracy unsure. embellished curvatures shapes make data set aesthetically appealing. think know real route — imagine recorded Flyer’s actual ship’s log — let know ’ll happily digitize can find time. happens, maintain aesthetic authentic routes data sets package.

title: Example geospatial data for cartography
text: just want data, two formats available: GeoPackage bundle — one zip containing .gpkg per data set: flyer-geodata.zip GeoJSON — individual .geojson files (one per data set), browseable data_dl/geojson/ layers EPSG:4326 (WGS 84). landcover_palette.csv file included formats maps landcover classes hex colors.

title: Example geospatial data for cartography
text: Brusca, Richard C. “1940 Ricketts-Steinbeck Sea Cortez Expedition, annotated lists species collection sites.” Journal Southwest 62, . 2 (2020): 218-334. freely available Sagarin, Raphael D., William F. Gilly, Charles H. Baxter, Nancy Burnett, Jon Christensen. “Remembering Gulf: changes marine communities Sea Cortez since Steinbeck Ricketts expedition 1940” Frontiers Ecology Environment 6, . 7 (2008): 372-379. open-access nearly final version Steinbeck, John. Log Sea Cortez. Penguin, 1995. Available local library, independent bookstore, Barnes & Noble near .

title: Example geospatial data for cartography
text: CONABIO CONANP, (09/2024). ‘Federal Protected Natural Areas Mexico, September 2024’, National Commission Knowledge Use Biodiversity. Mexico City, Mexico. GEBCO Compilation Group (2024) GEBCO 2024 Grid (doi:10.5285/1c44ce99-0a0d-5f4f-e063-7086abc0ea0f). Robinson, N., Regetz, J., Guralnick, R. P. (2014). EarthEnv-DEM90: nearly-global, void-free, multi-scale smoothed, 90m digital elevation model fused ASTER SRTM data. ISPRS Journal Photogrammetry Remote Sensing, 87:2014, 57-67. Available http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0924271613002360 Tuanmu, M.-N. W. Jetz. 2014. global 1-km consensus land-cover product biodiversity ecosystem modeling. Global Ecology Biogeography 23(9): 1031-1045.

what: A stylized bathymetric data set of the Sea of Cortez and adjacent areas. — bathymetry
title: A stylized bathymetric data set of the Sea of Cortez and adjacent areas. — bathymetry
text: stylized dataset bathymetric contours, intended mapping (analytical) applications. source raster aggregated, contoured, filtered small pieces, simplified, corner-cut, simplified . contours generated GEBCO Compilation Group (2024) GEBCO 2024 Grid (doi:10.5285/1c44ce99-0a0d-5f4f-e063-7086abc0ea0f).

title: A stylized bathymetric data set of the Sea of Cortez and adjacent areas. — bathymetry
code: bathymetry

title: A stylized bathymetric data set of the Sea of Cortez and adjacent areas. — bathymetry
text: sf tibble, single attribute giving elevation meters (depth stored negative elevation). elevation Elevation contour line, meters – negative values sea level. geometry sf linestring – bathymetric contour.

title: A stylized bathymetric data set of the Sea of Cortez and adjacent areas. — bathymetry
code: data(bathymetry)  library(ggplot2) ggplot() +  geom_sf(data = bathymetry, aes(color = elevation)) +  theme_minimal()

what: Species lists for collection sites — collections
title: Species lists for collection sites — collections
text: Species lists collections sites Brusco 2020 ('manuscipt')

title: Species lists for collection sites — collections
code: collections

title: Species lists for collection sites — collections
text: sf tibble one row per collection scientific_name Name collection per manuscript type_specimen Marked type based manuscript synonyms Synonym names mentioned manuscript other_notes notes collection manuscript questionable_id notes manuscript identification status site Collection site name per manuscript gbif_usage_key GBIF usage key integration GBIF gbif_scientific_name GBIF used scientific name collection gbif_match_type 'scientific_name' resolved GBIF gbif_status Whether GBIF recognizes particular name applied collection gbif_url Hyperlink GBIF page resolved entry. collection_site Collection site name per manuscript location_english flyer package name site 'places'

title: Species lists for collection sites — collections
code: data(collections) head(collections) #> # A tibble: 6 × 13 #>   type_specimen synonyms repository other_notes  questionable_id scientific_name #>   <chr>         <chr>    <chr>      <chr>        <chr>           <chr>           #> 1 NA            NA       NA         Hawksbill t… FALSE           Eretmochelys i… #> 2 NA            NA       NA         symbiotic h… FALSE           Obelia dichoto… #> 3 NA            NA       NA         pelagic cra… FALSE           Planes minutus  #> 4 NA            NA       NA         the pelagic… FALSE           Pleuroncodes p… #> 5 NA            NA       NA         the most co… FALSE           Geodia mesotri… #> 6 NA            NA       NA         one of the … FALSE           Aglaophenia di… #> # ℹ 7 more variables: gbif_usage_key <chr>, gbif_scientific_name <chr>, #> #   gbif_match_type <chr>, gbif_status <chr>, gbif_url <chr>, #> #   collection_site <chr>, location_english <chr>  ## if you want to make it spatial data(places) collections_sf <- dplyr::left_join(      collections,       dplyr::select(places, location_english, geometry),      by = 'location_english' ) |>   sf::st_as_sf()

what: Land and islands of Western Mexico — land
title: Land and islands of Western Mexico — land
text: simplified data set land polygons associated nations, adapted Natural Earth's global products. Built combining high-resolution land data set minor islands data set. Islands lacked administrative info Natural Earth assigned nearest nation. Based visual inspection match good; mismatch intentional – viva la Mexico god bless USA. "Made Natural Earth. Free vector raster map data @ naturalearthdata.com."

title: Land and islands of Western Mexico — land
code: land

title: Land and islands of Western Mexico — land
text: sf tibble, one multipolygon per country area. name Name country. geometry simplified multipolygon geometry.

title: Land and islands of Western Mexico — land
code: data(land)  library(ggplot2) ggplot() +  geom_sf(data = land, aes(fill = name)) +  theme_minimal()

what: Major landcover classes of Western Mexico — landcover
title: Major landcover classes of Western Mexico — landcover
text: simplified data set landcover types, derived global EarthEnv product. Tuanmu, M.-N. W. Jetz. 2014. global 1-km consensus land-cover product biodiversity ecosystem modeling. Global Ecology Biogeography 23(9): 1031-1045.

title: Major landcover classes of Western Mexico — landcover
code: landcover

title: Major landcover classes of Western Mexico — landcover
text: sf tibble one row per landcover polygon. class Name landcover class (slightly renamed source cases). geometry simplified polygon geometry.

title: Major landcover classes of Western Mexico — landcover
code: data(landcover)  library(ggplot2) ggplot() +  geom_sf(data = landcover, aes(fill = class)) +  theme_minimal()

what: Color palette for landcover data — lc_pal
title: Color palette for landcover data — lc_pal
text: hand-picked set hex colors (via coolors.co) intended pair reasonably landcover classes.

title: Color palette for landcover data — lc_pal
code: lc_pal

title: Color palette for landcover data — lc_pal
text: named character vector hex color codes, names matching levels landcover$class.

title: Color palette for landcover data — lc_pal
code: data(lc_pal) data(landcover)  library(ggplot2) ggplot() + geom_sf(data = landcover, aes(fill = class)) +  scale_fill_manual(values = lc_pal) +  theme_minimal()

what: Places the Western Flyer passed through on Ricketts and Steinbeck's journey to the Sea of Cortez. — places
title: Places the Western Flyer passed through on Ricketts and Steinbeck's journey to the Sea of Cortez. — places
text: dataset providing place names used book modern Mexican equivalents Spanish. Several book place names bit peculiar, localities match well current Spanish names.

title: Places the Western Flyer passed through on Ricketts and Steinbeck's journey to the Sea of Cortez. — places
code: places

title: Places the Western Flyer passed through on Ricketts and Steinbeck's journey to the Sea of Cortez. — places
text: sf tibble one row per stop. location_espanol current place name, Spanish. location_english place name used book. collect Logical. Whether crew collected specimens . date_arrive date Western Flyer arrived (passed place). date_depart date Western Flyer departed (passed place). geometry point geometry – perfectly accurate, often slightly offshore.

title: Places the Western Flyer passed through on Ricketts and Steinbeck's journey to the Sea of Cortez. — places
code: data(places)  library(ggplot2) ggplot() +  geom_sf(data = places, aes(color = date_arrive)) +  theme_minimal()

title: Protected areas of Mexico — protected
text: lightly simplified data set protected areas Mexico, cartographic purposes. CONABIO CONANP, (09/2024). 'Federal Protected Natural Areas Mexico, September 2024', National Commission Knowledge Use Biodiversity. Mexico City, Mexico.

title: Protected areas of Mexico — protected
code: protected

title: Protected areas of Mexico — protected
text: sf tibble, handful attributes briefly describing protected area. name Official name protected area. date_established Date preserve established. reserve_type locally defined category assigned reserve. Marine Terrestrial reserved areas 100pct surface category; Primarily Marine Primarily Terrestrial used mixed areas, based surface type dominates. geometry simplified multipolygon geometry.

title: Protected areas of Mexico — protected
code: data(protected)  library(ggplot2) ggplot() +  geom_sf(data = protected, aes(fill = reserve_type)) +  theme_minimal()

what: A stylized route of the Western Flyer on Ricketts and Steinbeck's journey to the Sea of Cortez. — route
title: A stylized route of the Western Flyer on Ricketts and Steinbeck's journey to the Sea of Cortez. — route
text: dataset one linestring segment per destination. Note route fictionalized book tweaked aesthetically pleasing, rather nautically accurate.

title: A stylized route of the Western Flyer on Ricketts and Steinbeck's journey to the Sea of Cortez. — route
code: route

title: A stylized route of the Western Flyer on Ricketts and Steinbeck's journey to the Sea of Cortez. — route
text: sf tibble, single attribute can joined places data set (desired). destination place name used book. Can joined places$location_english pick dates travel. geometry sf linestring, running previous destination current one.

title: A stylized route of the Western Flyer on Ricketts and Steinbeck's journey to the Sea of Cortez. — route
code: data(route)  places <- sf::st_drop_geometry(places) route <- dplyr::left_join(   route,   sf::st_drop_geometry(places),   by = c('destination' = 'location_english')   )   library(ggplot2) ggplot() +  geom_sf(data = route, aes(color = date_arrive)) +  theme_minimal()
